title: VPN for Business and Remote Work 2026: What Teams Actually Need
description: Remote workers and distributed teams have different VPN needs than individual users. Here is how to choose between consumer VPNs, business VPNs, and zero-trust alternatives.
content:
Consumer VPN vs. Business VPN

Consumer VPNs (NordVPN, ExpressVPN) protect individual internet traffic. Business VPNs (NordLayer, Perimeter81, Cisco AnyConnect) are designed for teams: they create encrypted tunnels to company resources, let IT manage access policies, and provide audit logs. If your goal is connecting remote employees to internal systems, a business VPN is the right tool. If individuals just need encrypted internet access while traveling, a consumer tier works.

What Business VPNs Offer

Centralized admin panel for user management. Role-based access control (who can reach which server or subnet). Activity logs for compliance. Multi-factor authentication integration. Site-to-site tunneling to connect office locations. These features are absent from consumer VPNs and are why enterprise tools cost more per user per month.

Zero Trust as an Alternative

Zero trust network access (ZTNA) tools like Cloudflare Access, Tailscale, and Zscaler Private Access are replacing traditional VPNs in many organizations. Instead of granting full network access through a VPN tunnel, ZTNA grants access per application, per user, per device. Lower attack surface, easier to manage at scale, and no performance bottleneck through a central VPN server. For teams starting from scratch, ZTNA is often the better architecture.
